text: Testing plays a vital role in software development by ensuring that code works as expected. Unit tests check small, individual parts of the program, such as a single function, to verify they behave correctly. Integration tests go further by testing how different parts of the system work together, catching issues that may appear only when components interact. A popular approach is test-driven development, where you write tests before the actual code. This helps you think carefully about requirements and design. Ultimately, thorough testing gives developers and stakeholders confidence that changes won't break existing functionality, making the software more reliable and easier to maintain over time.
